scope.summary: Update no-context model manual and backend navigation for lane launch, exchange, diagnostics, and recovery.
forbidden_paths: treating app/src legacy React/Tauri surfaces as canonical implementation authority
npm/JavaScript proof targets for this WP
app/src-tauri launch or IPC authority for this WP
proof commands that pass with zero matching tests
acceptance_criteria: Manual explains product purpose for model lanes, startup/launch commands, expected inputs/outputs, backend navigation, diagnostics, failure modes, and recovery.
Backend navigation exposes deterministic lane/run/message lookup paths for no-context models.
Manual references the validation harness and exact recovery proof surfaces.

MT-010 MUST update the canonical in-product UserManual authority rows/API/registry and any legacy ModelManual projection alias. Creating docs/model-manual content alone is insufficient. Proof MUST query/read the canonical UserManual surface and verify the legacy projection points to the same content/version.

MT-010 required_build_rules MUST include HBR-MAN-004 and HBR-INT-009. Manual/backend navigation entries MUST include Flight Recorder/EventLedger evidence paths and Flight Recorder/internal_diagnostics/Palmistry posture for each observable lane behavior, with absent internal_diagnostics/Palmistry marked DEFERRED-with-reason.

Manual/backend navigation covers lookup by run_id, lane_id, message_id, model_session_id/session_id, wp_id, mt_id, artifact_ref, ContextBundle id, Locus work-unit id, LoomBlock id, MemoryPack ref/hash, EventLedger event id, trace_id, span_id, and error code.

Manual self-consistency/freshness proof verifies every named command, IPC/API path, route, schema, storage/event contract, diagnostic surface, and recovery path exists or is explicitly tagged roadmap/deferred.

MT-010 owns Rust backend navigation/API lookup surfaces, not WP-wide manual coverage. Required navigation surfaces: run lookup, lane lookup, message lookup, artifact/context lookup, trace/span lookup, diagnostic tier lookup, and recovery lookup; each MUST have route/command id, input schema, output schema, error codes, EventLedger/Flight Recorder refs, and UserManual registry row.
The provisional diagnostics/navigation surface id for this WP is native_swarm_lane_diagnostics; it may later alias to the project-approved tool name without changing API semantics.
Remove any docs/model-manual or app/src grep as proof; manual/navigation proof must query Rust UserManual/API/storage/registry surfaces.

After implementation and before READY_FOR_VALIDATION, this MT MUST receive post-implementation adversarial review against the actual diff. Findings must be fixed or converted into explicit lifecycle blocker/missing_resource/proof_status state; the implementer may not self-certify COMPLETED.
scope.risk_if_missed: The feature remains usable only by models that remember this chat, which defeats recovery and parallel-agent operation.

implementation_notes: Manual/UserManual must explain ModelLaneRun, ModelLane, ModelLaneMessage, local/cloud/CLI/subagent launch paths, ContextBundle handoff, artifact promotion, cloud consent/projection, failure codes, and recovery.

Manual must use the project-approved internal viewing-tool name from the separate viewing-tool worktree/session and describe how a no-context model uses it or its backend navigation equivalent to inspect lane state.
Backend navigation must support deterministic lookup by run_id, lane_id, message_id, session_id/model_session_id, wp_id, mt_id, artifact_ref, and error code.
Manual entries must point at actual commands, IPC, routes, or backend functions; aspirational docs fail HBR-MAN-003.
If the project uses the legacy ModelManual name in code, update the existing in-product manual surface rather than creating a parallel documentation island.
Do not satisfy this MT by writing Markdown only; docs/model-manual may be a projection, but PostgreSQL/UserManual rows and API/navigation truth are the product surface.

Manual text must teach the layered pattern: models propose edits, Handshake validates/promotes deterministically, EventLedger/PostgreSQL stores authority, Flight Recorder records evidence, Loom/FEMS/ContextBundle provide retrievable context.
Manual entries for cloud/local/subagent/tool use must include consent/capability boundaries, expected inputs/outputs, common failure codes, and recovery steps.
Use Flight Recorder/EventLedger payloads as bounded events plus artifact refs; do not put unbounded model output, raw secrets, or prompt-only state in event payloads.

If this MT changes any product behavior, storage/event contract, command, IPC/API path, diagnostic surface, model lane, or navigation path, update the in-product UserManual in the same implementation change per HBR-MAN-004.

Product implementation for this MT must be Rust-only: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus surfaces where UI is touched. Legacy app/src, app/src-tauri, React, Tauri/WebView, TypeScript, JavaScript, docs/model-manual, and npm proof targets are reference-only or forbidden and MUST NOT satisfy implementation/proof authority.

Proof commands must name exact Rust test targets or exact test names. Zero matching tests is a hard failure. Deterministic provider fakes are allowed for cloud/local model calls, but PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT/UserManual/Argus authority must touch real Handshake-owned Rust resources or an explicit real PostgreSQL URL.
Each behavior-changing MT must include same-change UserManual coverage for the changed behavior; MT-011 audits coverage and cannot substitute for same-change HBR-MAN-004 proof.

This pre-implementation contract review does not replace post-implementation review. After coding, run adversarial review against the actual diff through the listed lenses and update MT status signaling before handoff.

risks: Manual can become stale if it describes aspirational paths instead of implemented navigation.
minimum_controls: Manual must cite implemented commands/API paths and backend navigation tests.

research_basis.authority_posture: Master spec/SPEC_CURRENT/product code are authority; HANDSHAKE_PRODUCT_REFERENCE.md and external research are navigation/evidence only.
product_code_evidence: src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/user_manual.rs exposes UserManual routes and legacy ModelManual alias mapping
patterns_applied: Typed multi-agent orchestration becomes ModelLaneRun/ModelLane/ModelLaneMessage records, never peer-to-peer chat authority.
Durable workflow/replay patterns become PostgreSQL/EventLedger aggregates, checkpoints, projections, and deterministic state transitions.
CRDT research becomes explicit draft/proposal/update/state-vector/snapshot/stale-base records before promotion.
Trace/span research becomes propagated trace_id/span_id/model_session_id/lane_id correlation across lanes, tools, providers, artifacts, and diagnostics.
Tool protocol research becomes Handshake capability check, consent/projection, Flight Recorder/EventLedger logging, execution, result capture, and recovery.
rejected_options: peer-to-peer model channels outside coordinator-owned ModelLaneMessage records
legacy app/src React/Tauri code as runtime authority
SQLite runtime/test/fixture/cache/fallback authority for this WP
Flight Recorder-only replay without PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority rows
manual or markdown prose as product runtime authority
research_basis.selected_handshake_translation: No-context manual/navigation must be backed by canonical UserManual rows and backend lookup APIs, with lane/run/message/ContextBundle/Loom/FEMS/Flight Recorder recovery paths verified against code truth.

handshake_compatibility.product_reference_file_posture: reference/navigation only; do not override SPEC_CURRENT, master spec, build rules, or product code evidence
handshake_compatibility.proposal_promotion_model: Models may propose, critique, summarize, and produce artifacts; Handshake validates/promotes deterministically through Rust backend authority surfaces before state becomes authoritative.
durable_authority: CRDT/write-box when shared editable state is touched
observability_required: HBR-INT-009 tier posture for Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, Palmistry
handshake_compatibility.no_context_model_requirement: A model with no chat history must be able to implement and verify this MT from this contract, SPEC_CURRENT/master spec, product code paths, UserManual/backend navigation, and listed proof targets.
handshake_compatibility.rust_only_implementation: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus only. TypeScript/React/app/src/app-src-tauri/npm/docs-model-manual are not implementation or proof authority for this WP.
handshake_compatibility.spec_realism_gate: Implementer may move only to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after exact Rust proof targets touch the named real resources; COMPLETED is validator-only.
